events.title: Israeli Air Force Major and Civilian Charged — First International Prediction Market Prosecution
events.body: Israeli prosecutors charge an Israeli Air Force major and a civilian for using classified military intelligence about planned IDF strikes on Iran to win more than $150,000 on Polymarket. The pair allegedly accessed nonpublic operational information weeks before the strikes, placed bets on their timing and outcome, and profited before Israeli authorities detected the trades. The case predates the American Van Dyke prosecution by more than two months and marks the first criminal charges anywhere in the world for prediction market insider trading using classified government information. Polymarket's Iran-strike markets had drawn over $239 million in total volume — a target worth pursuing for anyone with inside access.
events.source: Bloomberg, February 12, 2026; Times of Israel, February 12, 2026; Jerusalem Post, February 2026; Benzinga, February 2026.

events.title: $800 Million Polymarket Oil Trade — 15 Minutes Before Trump's Iran Announcement
events.body: At approximately 6:50 AM ET, an anonymous trader places roughly $800 million in oil futures contracts on Polymarket, betting oil prices will fall. Fifteen minutes later, President Trump posts on Truth Social about progress in Iran nuclear talks. Oil drops approximately 10%. The position generates an estimated $2–4 million in profit. Blockchain analytics firm Bubblemaps traces the transactions and tells CBS News: 'We think this will multiply from here.' Commodities whistleblower attorney David Kovel, speaking to 60 Minutes, states: 'Any trader right now is highly suspicious' — and that whoever placed the trade appeared to know the announcement was coming. The trade is first reported publicly in the CBS News 60 Minutes investigation, broadcast May 17, 2026. It is not an isolated incident.
events.source: CBS News / 60 Minutes, May 17, 2026; Senate Banking Committee letter to CFTC, April 9, 2026.

events.title: Landmark Lawsuit Filed
events.body: The Public Health Advocacy Institute files suit in the Court of Common Pleas of Philadelphia County against FanDuel, DraftKings, Genius Sports, and the National Football League. Named plaintiffs are Terry Thompson and Christopher Sage. The complaint calls the sportsbook apps "unreasonably dangerous products intentionally designed to maximize addiction." Case No. 260303384.
events.source: PHAI press release, March 24, 2026; Philadelphia Inquirer, March 30, 2026.

events.title: Federal Government Sues Three States to Shield Prediction Markets
events.body: The CFTC and US Department of Justice file simultaneous lawsuits against Connecticut, Arizona, and Illinois — all three had issued cease-and-desist orders against Kalshi and Polymarket for operating what they called illegal online sportsbooks. The CFTC seeks declaratory judgments that the Commodity Exchange Act gives the federal government exclusive jurisdiction over prediction market contracts, preempting state gambling law. Arizona had also filed criminal charges against Kalshi the prior month. Connecticut Attorney General William Tong accuses the Trump administration of "recycling industry arguments" rejected by district courts. The suits mark the first time the federal government has taken the affirmative position of legally protecting prediction market operators from state regulation — the same operators in which Donald Trump Jr. holds financial positions.
events.source: ABC News, April 2, 2026; Washington Post, April 2, 2026; PBS News, April 2, 2026; CBS Chicago, April 2, 2026; Norton Rose Fulbright legal analysis.

events.title: Second Oil Trade — $950 Million, Same Pattern, Three Weeks Before 60 Minutes Airs
events.body: A second anomalous oil futures position — approximately $950 million, larger than the March 23 trade — appears on Polymarket ahead of another Trump administration announcement about Iran. The trade follows the same pattern: a large bet on falling oil prices placed shortly before a Trump communication about Iran diplomacy moves the market. This trade is placed three weeks before CBS News 60 Minutes airs its report on the March 23 trade. Whoever placed it watched the first $800 million position generate an estimated $2–4 million in profit, saw that trade get reported on national television, and did it again — at larger scale. On April 9, the Senate Banking Committee writes to CFTC Chairman Selig demanding answers on both oil trades. The April 30 response deadline passes without public disclosure of any CFTC investigation.
events.source: CBS News / 60 Minutes, May 17, 2026; Senate Banking Committee letter to CFTC, April 9, 2026; Elizabeth Warren letter to CFTC, June 10, 2026.

events.title: Senate Banking Committee Demands Answers on Both Oil Trades
events.body: The Senate Banking Committee writes to CFTC Chairman Brian Selig demanding answers on both the March 23 ($800 million) and April 7 ($950 million) Polymarket oil trades. The letter asks whether the CFTC has opened investigations into either trade, what steps are being taken to identify the traders, and how the agency is responding to what the committee calls a pattern of apparent insider trading tied to presidential announcements. The deadline for Selig's response is April 30, 2026. No public response or investigation announcement follows by that date.
events.source: Senate Banking Committee letter to CFTC Chairman Selig, April 9, 2026; Elizabeth Warren letter to CFTC, June 10, 2026.

events.title: Kalshi Suspends Three Congressional Candidates
events.body: Kalshi announces it has suspended and fined one US Senate candidate and two House of Representatives candidates for what it calls "political insider trading." The three candidates had been betting on their own races.
events.source: CNBC, April 30, 2026; NBC News, May 1, 2026.

events.title: Van Dyke Indicted — First Federal Prediction Market Prosecution
events.body: US Army Special Forces Master Sergeant Gannon Ken Van Dyke, age 38, Fort Bragg, is indicted in the Southern District of New York for allegedly using classified intelligence about Operation Absolute Resolve, the mission to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, to win $410,000 on Polymarket. He wagered approximately $33,000 across 13 bets. The case marks the first time the CFTC has used the "Eddie Murphy Rule" to bring charges based on misuse of government information in a prediction market.
events.source: CNN Politics, CNBC, NBC News, Axios, April 23, 2026. Federal indictment unsealed SDNY.

events.title: Senate Unanimously Bans Members from Prediction Markets
events.body: The US Senate passes a unanimous voice-vote resolution banning senators and their staff from trading on prediction markets, effective immediately. The resolution is sponsored by Sen. Bernie Moreno (R-OH), with a staff-coverage amendment by Sen. Alex Padilla (D-CA). It comes eight days after the Kalshi suspensions.
events.source: CNBC, April 30, 2026; PBS News and NBC News, May 1, 2026.

events.title: 41 State Attorneys General Demand Federal Action on Prediction Markets
events.body: A bipartisan coalition of 41 state attorneys general writes to Congress and formally petitions the CFTC to clarify through rulemaking that it does not have jurisdiction over sports-related event contracts. The coalition argues that platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket are operating as unregulated sportsbooks in states that have not legalized sports betting. New York Attorney General Letitia James is among the signatories. The letter is the broadest coordinated state challenge yet to the federal government's claim that prediction markets fall outside state gambling law.
events.source: GamblingNews.com, May 6, 2026; SCCG Management, May 6, 2026; New York Attorney General press release.

events.title: Congress Demands Youth-Marketing Data from Ten Companies
events.body: Five members of Congress (Reps. Valerie Foushee, Paul Tonko, Betty McCollum, Kevin Mullin, and Sen. Richard Blumenthal) send letters to the CEOs of ten companies demanding data on how their platforms market to young adults aged 18 to 24. Written responses due by May 29. The ten companies: bet365, BetMGM, Caesars, DraftKings, Fanatics, FanDuel, Kalshi, Polymarket, PrizePicks, Robinhood.
events.source: Bettors Insider, May 13, 2026.

events.title: Schiff-Curtis Bipartisan Bill: Ban Sports Contracts on Prediction Markets
events.body: Senators Adam Schiff (D-CA) and John Curtis (R-UT) introduce the Prediction Markets Are Gambling Act, bipartisan legislation to prohibit CFTC-registered entities from listing any prediction contract that resembles a sports bet or casino-style game. The bill would return authority over sports betting-style contracts to states and tribal governments — the same position held by the coalition of 41 attorneys general. Schiff states the CFTC is "completely unequipped" to regulate the industry, and cites a Wall Street Journal analysis showing that approximately two-thirds of profits on prediction market exchanges are captured by just 0.1% of all traders.
events.source: Sen. Schiff press release, May 2026; CNBC, Squawk Box, May 2026; NBC News; Bettors Insider, May 13, 2026.

events.title: NFL Writes CFTC: Ban Manipulable Contracts, Raise Age to 21
events.body: NFL Senior Vice President Brendon Plack writes to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission requesting that prediction market platforms be prohibited from offering contracts on events that are "easily manipulable" by a single person — including first pass completions and individual field goal attempts — and on "inherently objectionable" events such as player injuries. The league asks the CFTC to raise the minimum trading age from 18 to 21 and ban margin trading on sports event contracts. The NFL's position is notable: the league remains the second-largest shareholder in Genius Sports, the data company that powers over 98% of the legal US sports betting market and earns a commission on every microbet placed.
events.source: CNBC, May 15, 2026; Casino Beats, May 18, 2026; ESPN; Sports Betting Dime, May 2026.

events.title: Colorado Passes First-in-Nation Daily Deposit Cap on Sports Betting Apps
events.body: The Colorado legislature passes Senate Bill 131 on a 20-15 vote and sends it to Governor Jared Polis, who has 30 days to sign or veto. The bill would make Colorado the first state in the country to cap daily deposits on sports betting platforms at six deposits per 24-hour period, ban credit card deposits entirely, and prohibit push notifications and text messages soliciting bets. Operators and their marketing affiliates would also be prohibited from targeting underage users with advertising. The bill passed on the final day of the 2026 legislative session.
events.source: Bettors Insider, May 18, 2026; Sports Betting Dime; SBC Americas; Colorado General Assembly SB26-131.

events.title: Minnesota Becomes First State to Ban Prediction Markets
events.body: Governor Tim Walz signs SF 4760 into law, making Minnesota the first US state to explicitly ban prediction markets. The law makes it a felony (up to 5 years in prison, $10,000 fine) to create, operate, manage, or advertise prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket. It also exposes banks, payment processors, media organizations, and sports leagues to criminal liability for providing services or data to these platforms. The Minnesota Senate passed the bill 56-10 and the House 100-32. The law takes effect August 1, 2026. AG Keith Ellison defended the law: "Prediction markets are designed to be addictive and prey especially on young people and low-income folks."
events.source: CBS News Minnesota, May 19, 2026; The Hill, May 20, 2026; Washington Examiner, May 20, 2026; CFTC.gov press release 9233-26.

events.title: CFTC and DOJ Sue Minnesota — Federal v. State Jurisdictional War Escalates
events.body: Less than 24 hours after Minnesota's prediction market ban becomes law, the CFTC and US Department of Justice file suit against the State of Minnesota, Governor Tim Walz, and state officials (CFTC v. State of Minnesota, No. 0:26-cv-02661, D. Minn.). CFTC Chairman Mike Selig states: "This Minnesota law turns lawful operators and participants in prediction markets into felons overnight." The federal government argues the Commodity Exchange Act gives the CFTC exclusive jurisdiction over prediction market contracts as federally regulated derivatives. The case, assigned to Judge Laura Provinzino in St. Paul, is expected to potentially reach the US Supreme Court. The CFTC has also sued Illinois, Arizona, and Connecticut over similar state-level prediction market restrictions, making this the central regulatory war of 2026.
events.source: CFTC.gov Press Release 9233-26, May 19, 2026; The Hill, May 20, 2026; Yahoo Finance, May 20, 2026; The Block, May 20, 2026.

events.title: Senate Hearing: Prediction Market Firms Take Two Hours of Direct Fire
events.body: The Senate Commerce Subcommittee on Consumer Protection, Technology, and Data Privacy holds "No Sure Bets: Protecting Sports Integrity in America" at the Dirksen Senate Office Building, 10:00 a.m. ET. Chair: Sen. Marsha Blackburn (R-TN). Harry Levant, Director of Gambling Policy at PHAI, testifies that the products are "a known addictive product, just like heroin" and describes an "avalanche of unregulated advertising" targeting young people. Sen. John Hickenlooper tells prediction market executives they are "unleashing the hounds of hell" through social media. Sen. Ted Cruz enters active match-fixing cases into the record — NBA players and coaches accused of manipulating performance, two MLB pitchers allegedly rigging pitches for money, MLS players deliberately getting yellow cards to win bets. Cruz states "The Supreme Court may have to decide" whether the CFTC or the states have jurisdiction over sports prediction market contracts. It is the first time any Senate body has directly addressed prediction markets and their intersection with sports wagering.
events.source: CoinDesk, May 20, 2026; PR Newswire (PHAI); Philadelphia Inquirer, May 19, 2026; Senate Commerce Committee official record.

events.title: House Oversight Committee Opens Formal Investigation into Polymarket and Kalshi
events.body: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer (R-KY) launches a formal congressional investigation into Polymarket and Kalshi, sending document requests to both companies' CEOs — Shayne Coplan at Polymarket and Tarek Mansour at Kalshi. The requests seek information about the companies' ownership structures, their relationships with the Trump family, any communications with the administration, and what steps they have taken to prevent insider trading on their platforms. The deadline for document production is June 5, 2026. The investigation marks the first formal House inquiry into prediction market operators — and comes from the Republican-controlled committee, not the Democratic minority, underscoring the bipartisan nature of congressional concern.
events.source: House Oversight Committee, May 22, 2026; CNBC, April 15, 2026.

events.title: Google Engineer Charged — $1.2M Profit Using Confidential Company Data on Polymarket
events.body: Michele Spagnuolo, a software engineer at Google, is charged with commodities fraud, wire fraud, and money laundering for allegedly using Google's confidential internal "Year in Search" data — which the company closely guards until its annual year-end release — to place $2.7 million in wagers on Polymarket and profit approximately $1.2 million. Prosecutors allege Spagnuolo accessed the data through his position at Google and used it to predict with unusual accuracy which search terms and events would dominate public interest, then bet accordingly. The case extends the "Eddie Murphy Rule" framework — previously applied to Van Dyke's use of classified military intelligence — to corporate insiders with access to material non-public business information. The CFTC's enforcement priority on prediction market insider trading has now produced two prosecutions. Neither involves the $800M or $950M oil futures trades.
events.source: DOJ press release, May 27, 2026; CFTC enforcement action, May 27, 2026.

events.title: Youth-Marketing Data Deadline Reaches Congress
events.body: The May 29 deadline passes for ten companies to deliver written responses and youth-marketing data to Congress. The data covers how FanDuel, DraftKings, Kalshi, Polymarket, bet365, BetMGM, Caesars, Fanatics, PrizePicks, and Robinhood have marketed to adults aged 18 to 24. This data is potentially the documentary equivalent of the internal tobacco industry research — the first time Congress may have internal records of how these platforms targeted young adults. Whether all ten companies complied fully, and what the data reveals, is an open question as of the date of this publication.
events.source: Bettors Insider, May 13, 2026; Congress letter, May 11, 2026.

events.title: Colorado Governor Signs First-in-Nation Sports Betting Deposit Cap Into Law
events.body: Governor Jared Polis signs SB 26-131, making Colorado the first state in the country to impose a statutory daily deposit cap on sports betting platforms. The law limits bettors to six deposits per 24-hour period, bans credit card deposits entirely, and prohibits push notifications and text messages soliciting bets. Operators and affiliates are also barred from targeting users under 21 with advertising, and from using marketing language like "bonus bet" or "no sweat." The law takes effect August 12, 2026. Colorado becomes the template other states are watching.
events.source: Colorado Politics, June 2, 2026; Colorado Springs Gazette, June 2, 2026; SCCG Management, June 4, 2026; RotoWire, June 2026.

events.title: George Santos Under Federal Investigation for Insider Trading on Kalshi
events.body: CNN and NPR report that former Rep. George Santos is under federal investigation by the CFTC — and potentially the DOJ — for allegedly using his access to insider political information to profit on Kalshi. On February 23, Santos publicly posted "I'll be in the gallery" for Trump's State of the Union, sending Kalshi odds of his attendance surging to 75%. He did not attend. Three sources with direct knowledge of his trades told NPR he had been betting against himself the entire time, walking away with profits in the tens of thousands. Kalshi flagged Santos to the DOJ and CFTC after detecting the suspicious trades. The case marks the first political insider-trading investigation on a prediction market platform — and involves a platform on which Donald Trump Jr. serves as a paid strategic advisor.
events.source: CNN Politics, June 2, 2026; NPR, June 2, 2026; ABC News, June 2, 2026; Newsweek, June 2026; PlayUSA.

events.title: Van Dyke Pleads Not Guilty — Trial Set for December 7
events.body: Gannon Ken Van Dyke, the US Army Special Forces Master Sergeant indicted in April for using classified intelligence about the Maduro capture to win $410,000 on Polymarket, pleads not guilty at his pretrial conference in the Southern District of New York. He is released on $250,000 bail with travel restricted to portions of New York, North Carolina, California, and points necessary to travel between them. The court sets December 7, 2026 as the trial date — making Van Dyke's case the first prediction market insider trading prosecution to go to trial in US history. The question of who else with classified access has placed similar bets and has not yet been caught remains open. A Defense Department source told 60 Minutes that Van Dyke was "just a small fish" and that "dozens more" cases may exist.
events.source: PBS News, June 2026; Time, April 24, 2026; CNBC, April 23, 2026; DOJ Office of Public Affairs; CBS News / 60 Minutes, May 17, 2026.

events.title: Warren Demands CFTC Records on Both Oil Trades — Deadline June 18
events.body: Senator Elizabeth Warren writes to CFTC Chairman Brian Selig demanding records on both Polymarket oil futures trades — the $800 million position on March 23 and the $950 million position on April 7 — and answers about whether any investigation has been opened. Warren's letter sets a June 18, 2026 deadline for Selig's response. The letter notes that the Senate Banking Committee wrote to the CFTC on April 9 with the same questions and received no substantive response by the April 30 deadline. Warren writes that the two trades, totaling approximately $1.75 billion in combined positions, represent a pattern that demands explanation. The June 18 deadline falls eight days after the letter's date.
events.source: Sen. Elizabeth Warren letter to CFTC Chairman Selig, June 10, 2026.

events.title: CFTC Releases First Proposed Rules for Prediction Markets
events.body: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission releases its first proposed regulatory framework for prediction markets. The CFTC would permit most sports event contracts: moneylines, point spreads, game totals, and player props. It would prohibit contracts it considers too granular or manipulable: next-pitch outcomes, next-play call, officiating decisions, and markets on sports below the collegiate level. The proposed rules do not address the addiction and public-health dimensions of the platforms at all. The framework arrives while the CFTC is simultaneously suing four states (CT, AZ, IL, MN) to block state-level regulation of the same platforms. Prediction market traders currently assign 64% odds that the Supreme Court will accept a sports event contract case before the end of 2026.
events.source: Covers.com, June 2026; Sportico, June 2026; Front Office Sports, June 2026; Arnold Ventures analysis.

events.title: PHAI Lawsuit Enters Discovery
events.body: The Public Health Advocacy Institute lawsuit moves into discovery this summer. Internal documents from FanDuel, DraftKings, and Genius Sports will be subject to subpoena. The discovery process may surface internal communications about VIP host strategy, micro-bet design, and addiction data that have never been public. Nationwide class action suits continue to pile up on behalf of young adults and families who lost loved ones to gambling-related suicide.
events.source: PHAI press release, March 24, 2026.
